steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Text Structure and Purpose question. The underlined portion follows Morales's claim that our understanding of walking decisions is "insufficiently robust." The task is to identify what role the underlined clause plays relative to that claim.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It illustrates a scholarly disagreement described earlier in the sentence.: the underlined portion lists studies emphasizing different factors (norms, sidewalk width), but these are not shown to be in conflict with each other. The sentence presents variety in research focus, not a dispute. ✗
  • Choice B — It summarizes the findings of studies whose methods are discussed earlier in the sentence.: the sentence never describes the methods of any study; it only names different factors the studies emphasize. There is nothing to summarize about methods. ✗
  • Choice C — It elaborates on a claim presented earlier in the sentence.: the underlined portion expands on what "insufficiently robust" means in practice — different studies focus on isolated factors while walking decisions actually involve multiple conditions at once. This is a direct elaboration of Morales's claim. ✓
  • Choice D — It acknowledges a potential objection to the argument presented earlier in the sentence.: an objection would push back against Morales's claim. The underlined portion instead supports and develops it by showing why single-factor studies are insufficient. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
